mechanism_narrative: Industrial production drives economic output expectations. Rising INDPRO indicates expanding manufacturing and supply capacity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Extreme unemployment above 10% signals severe economic distress and depressed consumer demand. When UNRATE exceeds this threshold, expectations for corporate earnings collapse and the equity risk premium surges, pushing S&P 500 prices lower over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot value is 13.3, well above 10% threshold
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in all months when UNRATE exceeds 10%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of low inflation expectations and near-zero interest rates creates negative real interest rates. This dynamic pushes investors out of cash and bonds into equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns. The signal emerges from the interaction of a high CPI level and a very low policy rate.
evidence_refs: fact: current FEDFUNDS is 0.05 and CPIAUCSL is 255.8, implying negative real rates
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * (1-FEDFUNDS)) fails to produce a statistically significant positive slope (p > 0.10) in a 5-year rolling regression of 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive GDP growth as measured by GDPC1 confirms the economy is in an early expansion phase. In this regime, rising aggregate demand drives corporate sales and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal activates only when the economy has entered a recovery from a downturn.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate is above 0% (i.e., positive)
evidence_refs: fact: last quarterly GDPC1 level was 18974.7 (need to compute growth from prior quarter)
regime_hint: early economic expansion following a recession
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return is not positive and significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) in all periods where GDPC1 quarterly growth turns positive after a negative quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a direct measure of labor market health. Increases in PAYEMS signal rising income and consumption, which directly support corporate earnings and equity valuations. This trend is always active and predicts positive short-term returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month changes in PAYEMS and the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is not consistently positive; rank IC falls below 0.02 in walk-forward out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for construction activity and durable goods demand. When HOUST recovers from very low levels and crosses above the 1,000,000-unit annualized threshold, it signals a housing market trough and subsequent economic recovery, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 1000 (thousands) from a lower level
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot value is 891, below the 1000 threshold
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following HOUST crossing above 1000 is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of retail sales (RSAFS) and non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) captures consumer-driven economic momentum. When both series rise together, it indicates robust household income and spending, creating a virtuous cycle for corporate earnings. The joint signal is more powerful than either in isolation and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The predictive power of the interaction term (RSAFS growth * PAYEMS growth) is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression controlling for each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of high and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL monthly growth above 0.3%), the Federal Reserve is forced to tighten monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations. The signal is conditional on entering this high-inflation regime and predicts negative returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact: current CPIAUCSL level is 255.8, need prior month to compute growth
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) in all months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth is above 0.3%.
